zh.lifeSummary: 當Judith Ni ÓNéill於1492年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 泰龍卡萬時，她的父親Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain32歲，母親Lady Eleanor FitzGerald32歲。她於1514年與Manus  Mhor O'Donnell在英國北愛爾蘭庫迪 泰龍斯特拉巴結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1535年8月在愛爾蘭庫迪 多聶加爾享年43歲。

nameMeanings.name: Ni
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Pei-Lin, Kong, Min, Ming, Yung, Bin, Cao, Huang, Jing, Wen, Zhen, Biao, Chang, Chi, Quan, Yaping.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 倪, meaning ‘boundary’ or ‘infants’ in ancient Chinese: adopted in order to escape from a feud in place of the Chinese surname 郳, which is also pronounced Ni. The original surname was from Ni (郳), the name of an ancient state, also called Little Zhu (located in Shandong province), granted to the son of Duke Wu of Zhu (ruler of the state of Zhu, died 796  BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 賴, see   Lai   1.

variants.name: Lai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ching, Kwok, Ming, Wing, Wai, Wen, Chi, Chun, Ho, Kam, Man, Yee.  Vietnamese Hung, Thanh, Tuan, Hoa, Ky, Lan, Thang, Tien, Trang, Tu, Yen, Anh.

Chinese: Mandarin or Cantonese form of the surname 賴, meaning ‘depend on’ in Chinese: from Lai (賴), the name of a state (located in Henan province), which was annexed by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 來, meaning ‘to come’ in Chinese: (i) simplified form of the placename Lai (郲), the name of a fief (located in Henan province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) adopted in place of the Chinese surname Lai (萊) which originated from the name of a fief/state. After its annihilation by the state of Qi, people simplified the name as 萊 in order to escape from political persecutions.

variants.name: Ny
meanings: Swedish: ornamental or soldier's name from ny ‘new’. It may sometimes derive from a placename beginning with Ny-.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 倪 (see   Ni  ) or 聶 (see   Nie   2).

nameMeanings.name: ÓNéill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan, Dermot, Liam, Nuala, Bridie, Brigid, Conor, Fintan, John Patrick.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Néill ‘descendant of Niall’, an Irish personal name possibly derived from néal ‘cloud’ (see Neil ).

History: Bearers of this surname mostly claim descent from Niall Noígíallach (Niall of the Nine Hostages), a legendary king of Ireland who flourished in the 5th century. His descendants, the Ui Neill dynasty, dominated Ireland, on and off, from the 6th to the 10th century. The surname itself can be traced with some confidence to one of these descendants, namely Niall Glúndubh mac Aodha (died 919), High King of Ireland from 911 to 919, with a power base in Tyrone, Ulster.

variants.name: O'Neall
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: McNelis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, Brendan.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Niallghuis ‘son of Niallghus’, a personal name (intended as complimentary) composed of the personal name Niall (see O'Neill ) + gus ‘vigor’. In Irish the combination of [k] and [n] was generally pronounced [kr], Anglicized as the variants McCreless and McCrillis .

variants.name: O'Neal
meanings: Irish (Longford, Down): variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: O'Niel
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: Odneal
meanings: Perhaps an irregular variant of Irish   O'Neill  .

variants.name: Nealon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Declan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Nialláin ‘descendant of Niallán’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of Niall, see O'Neill , Neil .

variants.name: O'Neil
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Conn, Donal, Liam, Siobhan, Aileen, Eamon, Michael Patrick, Seamus, Sean Patrick, William Sean.
Irish (Mayo, Tipperary): variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: Neil
meanings: Scottish, English (Lancashire), and Irish (Munster): from the Gaelic personal name Niall (genitive Néill), possibly derived from Irish néal ‘cloud’. In most cases the name probably represents a shortened form of the derived patronymics McNeill or the comparable Irish O'Neill .
English: variant of   Neal  .

variants.name: O'Neel
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Neill  .

nameMeanings.name: Judith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘Jewess’ or ‘woman from Judea’, borne by a Jewish heroine whose story is recorded in the Book of Judith in the Apocrypha. Judith is portrayed as a beautiful widow who delivers her people from the invading Assyrians by gaining the confidence of their commander, Holofernes, and cutting off his head while he is asleep; without their commander, the Assyrians are duly routed. This has been a perennially popular Jewish name. In the English-speaking world it was taken up in the 16th century, having been in occasional use among Gentiles before this: for example, it was borne by a niece of William the Conqueror. It enjoyed great popularity between the 1940s and the 1960s. Today's notable bearers include the American novelist  Judith Krantz  (  b. 1928 ) and the Scottish composer  Judith Weir  (  b. 1954 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Judith Ni ÓNéill was born in 1492, in Cavan, County Tyrone, Ireland, her father, Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain, was 32 and her mother, Lady Eleanor FitzGerald, was 32. She married Manus  Mhor O'Donnell in 1514, in Strabane, County Tyrone, Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in August 1535, in County Donegal, Ireland, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Judith Ni ÓNéill est née en 1492, à Cavan, County Tyrone, Irlande, son père, Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Lady Eleanor FitzGerald, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Manus  Mhor O'Donnell en 1514, à Strabane, County Tyrone, Irlande du Nord, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en août 1535, à County Donegal, Irlande, à l’âge de 43 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Judith Ni ÓNéill(이)가 1492년 아일랜드 카운티 타이론 캐반에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Eleanor FitzGerald(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1514년 영국 북아일랜드 카운티 타이론 스트라반에서 Manus  Mhor O'Donnell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1535년 8월 아일랜드 카운티 도네걸에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Judith Ni ÓNéill родилась в 1492 г. в местности каван, коунти тироун, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Eleanor FitzGerald, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Manus  Mhor O'Donnell в 1514 г. в местности страбан, коунти тироун, Северная Ирландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла август 1535 г. в местности коунти доунегаль, Ирландия в возрасте 43 лет.
